channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Humans' pull toward alcohol may have ancient origins (according to chimp pee)
item.description: Scientists learned that wild African chimpanzees consume alcohol by eating fermented fruit, suggesting that human attraction to alcohol may have ancient evolutionary origins.
item.content:encoded: Scientists learned that wild African chimpanzees consume alcohol by eating fermented fruit, suggesting that human attraction to alcohol may have ancient evolutionary origins.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: The U.S. and Israeli war on Iran is straining relations with some U.S. allies
item.description: President Trump criticized the U.K. over its stance on the war on Iran, as he praised and welcomed Germany's chancellor to the White House.
item.content:encoded: President Trump criticized the U.K. over its stance on the war on Iran, as he praised and welcomed Germany's chancellor to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Who will succeed Khamenei?
item.description: Iran's supreme leader is dead, but the regime endures. Iran scholar Mehrzad Boroujerdi walks through how the leadership succession could unfold.
item.content:encoded: Iran's supreme leader is dead, but the regime endures. Iran scholar Mehrzad Boroujerdi walks through how the leadership succession could unfold.
item.dc:creator: Connor Donevan

item.title: Israel marks somber Purim under Iranian missile fire
item.description: Israeli Jews are celebrating Purim and drawing parallels with the war in Iran and the ancient biblical story.
item.content:encoded: Israeli Jews are celebrating Purim and drawing parallels with the war in Iran and the ancient biblical story.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Roger Bennett's world revolves around the World Cup – and it always has
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers talks to Roger Bennett about his new book, We Are the World (Cup), and what could be in store in this year's tournament as America hosts for the first time in 32 years.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers talks to Roger Bennett about his new book, We Are the World (Cup), and what could be in store in this year's tournament as America hosts for the first time in 32 years.
item.dc:creator: Juana Summers

item.title: President Trump aimed to cut science funding. Congress has quietly restored much of it
item.description: Despite President Trump's efforts to deeply cut science funding from the federal budget in 2026, Congress quietly restored much of the funding to previous levels in recent weeks.
item.content:encoded: Despite President Trump's efforts to deeply cut science funding from the federal budget in 2026, Congress quietly restored much of the funding to previous levels in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Pregnant migrant girls are being sent to a Texas shelter flagged as medically risky
item.description: Government officials and advocates for the children worry the goal is to concentrate them in Texas, where abortion is banned.
item.content:encoded:
Government officials and advocates for the children worry the goal is to concentrate them in Texas, where abortion is banned.

(Image credit: Patricia Lim)
item.dc:creator: Mark Betancourt

item.title: Trump administration reverses course on law firms, vowing to appeal
item.description: The Justice Department reversed course and took back an effort to abandon an appeal against four big law firms that challenged President Trump's punitive executive orders.
item.content:encoded: The Justice Department reversed course and took back an effort to abandon an appeal against four big law firms that challenged President Trump's punitive executive orders.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: U.S. gasoline prices set to jump with Iran war
item.description: The U.S. war with Iran is rattling energy and financial markets. Gasoline prices jumped overnight while stock prices tumbled.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. war with Iran is rattling energy and financial markets. Gasoline prices jumped overnight while stock prices tumbled.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Hezbollah attack on Israel drags fragile Lebanon back into war
item.description: Tensions in Lebanon are soaring as Hezbollah renews its attacks on Israel.
item.content:encoded: Tensions in Lebanon are soaring as Hezbollah renews its attacks on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf
